scenarios.description: children() returns empty list when element has no child nodes (childNodeSize()==0 branch)
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("div"); // no children

scenarios.description: children() returns single-element list when one Element child present (childNodeSize()>0 and one loop iteration)
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("div"); el.appendChild(new Element("p"));
scenarios.expectedResult:
returned Elements contains exactly the child

element
pseudocode: Element el = new Element("div"); el.appendChild(new Element("p"));

scenarios.description: children() filters out non-Element nodes: only Element children returned when mixed nodes present
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("div"); el.appendChild(new TextNode("text")); el.appendChild(new Element("span"));
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Elements contains only the <span> element and excludes TextNode
pseudocode: Element el = new Element("div"); el.appendChild(new TextNode("x")); el.appendChild(new Element("span"));

scenarios.description: children() invalidates and rebuilds cache after node list change (nodelistChanged resets shadowChildrenRef)
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("div"); el.appendChild(new Element("a")); el.children(); // prime cache
scenarios.expectedResult: after appending new child <b>, el.children() returns two elements in correct order
pseudocode: Element el = new Element("div"); el.appendChild(new Element("a")); el.children();
assert result.size() == 2; assert result.get(0).tagName().equals("a"); assert result.get(1).tagName().equals("b");
